narrative: Markets are navigating a sharp divergence between fundamental and technical signals today. Geopolitical risk from escalating US-Iran tensions is underpinning a broad safe-haven USD bid, with oil's surge toward WTI $100 adding further pressure on commodity and EM currencies — a theme reinforced by FOMC anticipation keeping dollar bulls engaged. Yet the technical picture tells a starkly contradictory story: the USD ranks as one of the weakest currencies on the momentum scorecard, while AUD and CAD — both fundamentally pressured by risk-off sentiment and oil volatility — register as the two strongest pairs technically, with AUDUSD showing aligned Buy signals on both Daily and H4 timeframes. USDCAD reflects a similar tension, with H4 pointing to USD weakness despite bearish CAD fundamentals. EUR and GBP remain sidelined ahead of pivotal ECB and BoE decisions, while BoJ hawkishness offers JPY support that USD strength continues to cap. **Key watch point:** AUDUSD's technical-fundamental contradiction — any deterioration in risk sentiment or oil-driven AUD selling could trigger a sharp technical unwind, making this pair today's primary inflection point.

themes.theme: US-Iran tensions and oil price surge supporting USD safe-haven bid
Oil price spike (WTI eyeing $100, Hormuz closure risk) pressuring oil-importing EM/Asian FX
BoJ hawkish signals with June hike prospects, but JPY paring gains on USD strength
Gold and silver weakening on strong USD ahead of FOMC, risk-off not fully translating to metals
EUR range-bound ahead of ECB decision; inflation expectations rising
GBP steady ahead of BoE rate decision, EUR/GBP flat
Commodity and risk-sensitive currencies under pressure (NZD, AUD, CAD)
China vows domestic demand expansion – modest CNY/Asia support but offset by oil risk
FOMC meeting in focus as key near-term catalyst

key_observation: Geopolitical risk premium from US-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz closure fears is the dominant driver, sending WTI toward $100, boosting USD broadly, pressuring metals despite risk-off tone, and weighing heavily on oil-importing EM currencies like INR. BoJ hawkishness provides a JPY floor but USD strength is overwhelming. All eyes now on FOMC for next directional cue.
watch: Any escalation/de-escalation in Strait of Hormuz situation; FOMC statement tone on inflation given oil shock; BoE and ECB rate decisions; Australian CPI print as AUD catalyst
